mappings.rationaleAI: Education covering aims, ideologies, learning theories, teaching methods, and levels from early childhood through tertiary directly addresses philosophies of education (A), the learning process and teaching art (B), and the organization of education (C).
Schools as institutions providing structured learning from preschool through secondary directly address preschool education (C.1.a) and elementary and secondary education (C.1.b).
Universities as tertiary institutions awarding degrees across multiple disciplines and combining teaching with research directly address higher education (C.1.c).
Learning as acquisition of knowledge through experience, instruction, and observation, spanning associative and complex forms, directly addresses processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
AI as computer systems performing tasks associated with human intelligence connects to instructional media including computer-based instruction (B.2.d).
Libraries as organized collections supporting learning through books, media, and databases connect to instructional media and informational aids (B.2.d).
Nursing education through professional programs and advanced practice credentials connects to higher education and professional schools (C.1.c) and vocational training (C.1.f).
Psychology's study of cognition, development, motivation, and personality connects to experimental findings and theories on processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
Medicine's education and licensing through professional schools directly addresses higher education and professional schools (C.1.c).
Play's role in cognitive development, social learning, and emotional regulation connects to processes of learning and thinking (B.1).
Computers as programmable machines enabling digital learning and internet-based education connect to instructional media including computer-based instruction (B.2.d).
Writing as a visual representation of language enabling communication across time connects to instructional media and aids for educational purposes (B.2.d).
Oral tradition as the transmission of knowledge through speech and performance connects to speaking-listening facilities as instructional media (B.2.d).
The Enlightenment's emphasis on reason, empirical inquiry, and rational reform shaped philosophies of education prioritizing critical thinking and individual development (A).
Tolstoy's educational philosophy and advocacy for non-coercive pedagogy contribute to philosophies of education (A).
Kant's epistemology and ethics, including his views on rational autonomy and moral development, contribute to philosophies of education (A).
The Catholic Church's central role in Western education through law, universities, and philosophy connects to the education of teachers and educational institutions (C.2.a).
Museums as public educational institutions displaying cultural and scientific objects connect to visual and observational aids in instruction (B.2.c).
Video games as interactive electronic systems with educational applications connect to computer-based instruction as instructional media (B.2.d).
Communication as the transmission of meaning through coded messages connects to pedagogy and the components of the teaching situation (B.2).
